positioning.summary: A Full-Stack Systems & DevOps Engineer with 4 years of hands-on experience designing, building, deploying, and scaling production-grade systems. Delivers complete solutions across frontend, backend, distributed processing, cloud infrastructure, and DevOps automation—turning ideas into reliable, observable, and highly operable platforms. Core strengths include end-to-end systems ownership, reliability-first engineering, operational excellence, and the ability to evolve architectures from lean foundations to distributed systems as business needs grow.

systems_ownership: Deliver end-to-end solutions from application development through cloud infrastructure, deployment, monitoring, and production operations
Take complete responsibility for the entire system lifecycle
balanced_evolution: Start with lean architectures for rapid delivery
Evolve systems incrementally as business needs and operational demands grow
Apply evolutionary architecture approach from Monolith → Modular Services → Distributed Systems when justified by scale
reliability_first_engineering: Design systems with fault tolerance, idempotency, retry strategies, graceful degradation, and recovery mechanisms as foundational principles
Build for resilience using failure isolation, retry mechanisms, checkpoint recovery
operational_excellence: Build observable, maintainable, and automated production environments
Implement CI/CD, Infrastructure as Code, monitoring, and reproducible deployments
Focus on production operability from day one

quantifiable_impact: Designed a distributed scraping platform with 20 parallel workers, reducing complete extraction and processing cycles to approximately 5 minutes per batch
Reduced infrastructure provisioning to a single-command deployment through Infrastructure as Code
Designed stateless architectures enabling horizontal scaling and zero-downtime deployments
Implemented comprehensive observability practices that significantly improved production visibility and reduced issue detection time

system_design_and_architecture: Design architectures driven by business requirements, operational constraints, and expected system growth
Maintain clear separation between presentation, business logic, data, and infrastructure layers
Design for resilience using failure isolation, retry mechanisms, checkpoint recovery, and graceful degradation
application_engineering: Design responsive web applications and real-time user experiences with maintainability and scalability in mind
Build secure backend services, APIs, authentication systems, and business workflows
Prioritize clean architecture, modularity, testability, and long-term maintainability
distributed_systems_engineering: Design asynchronous and event-driven systems for scalable background processing
Build resilient processing pipelines capable of handling failures and retries
Engineer distributed worker architectures that improve throughput while maintaining reliability
Apply queue-based communication patterns to decouple services and improve scalability
data_engineering_and_automation: Design automated data collection and processing workflows
Build extraction, transformation, validation, and enrichment pipelines for structured data
Develop browser automation and workflow automation solutions that replace repetitive manual processes
Focus on data quality, consistency, and operational reliability throughout processing pipelines

experience.title: DevSecOps & Full-Stack Engineer
experience.overview: Design and implement cloud-native backend infrastructure, CI/CD pipelines, automation systems, distributed backend services, and production deployment workflows.
responsibilities: Design and implement cloud-native backend infrastructure on AWS
Build and maintain CI/CD pipelines using Jenkins and GitLab CI/CD
Develop Infrastructure as Code solutions using CloudFormation and Terraform
Configure containerized deployments using Docker and AWS ECS
Build event-driven backend services using AWS SNS, SQS, and EventBridge
Implement observability using CloudWatch, OpenTelemetry, Prometheus, Grafana, and Loki
Automate software delivery, deployment, monitoring, and operational workflows
Improve security, deployment reliability, and production automation

experience.title: Frontend Engineer
experience.overview: Develop modern React and React Native applications for multiple client projects, focusing on user experience, reusable components, API integration, and scalable frontend architecture.
responsibilities: Develop React and React Native applications
Build reusable UI components and application layouts
Integrate REST APIs and backend services
Implement authentication and role-based workflows
Manage application state and frontend architecture
Optimize application performance and responsiveness
Collaborate with backend developers and designers

experience.title: React Native Developer
experience.overview: Develop a React Native mobile commerce application integrated with a headless Shopify backend.
responsibilities: Develop mobile application features using React Native
Integrate Shopify GraphQL APIs
Build product catalog and order management workflows
Synchronize inventory and order information
Improve application performance and usability
Collaborate with backend and product teams

experience.title: MERN Stack Developer
experience.overview: Work on full-stack web applications across e-commerce, restaurant management, and financial workflow systems using the MERN stack.
responsibilities: Develop backend APIs using Node.js and Express
Build React-based frontend applications
Design MongoDB data models
Implement authentication and authorization
Integrate third-party services and cloud storage
Develop workflow-based business applications
featured_projects: Tax Rebate Processing & Refund Management Platform

experience.title: Freelance Full-Stack MERN Developer
experience.overview: Deliver freelance MERN stack projects for international clients, building responsive web applications and REST APIs while managing complete project lifecycles from requirements to deployment.
responsibilities: Build full-stack MERN applications
Develop REST APIs using Node.js and Express
Build React user interfaces
Design MongoDB database schemas
Deploy client applications
Maintain client communication and project delivery

projects.name: Tender Discovery & Procurement Intelligence Platform
projects.description: A distributed, state-driven ingestion and processing pipeline for automated tender procurement intelligence. The system replaces manual tender tracking across multiple portals with a fault-tolerant distributed execution engine that processes procurement data through controlled processing states.
business_impact: Automated discovery of procurement opportunities across multiple portals
Significant reduction in manual monitoring effort
Faster identification of relevant tenders with improved accuracy
Centralized procurement intelligence database
Scalable monitoring across large number of sources
engineering_highlights: Multi-stage pipeline architecture: Portal Registry → Seed URL Discovery → Page Crawling → Page Classification → Tender Detection → Data Extraction → PCI & Relevance Scoring → Document Enrichment → Intelligence Repository
Distributed worker architecture using Celery task queues and AWS ECS worker clusters
Strategy-based extraction system with pluggable portal-specific parsing strategies
State-driven job lifecycle model (PENDING → PROCESSING → COMPLETED/FAILED) for fault tolerance
Control plane, execution plane, data ingestion plane, processing plane, intelligence plane, and persistence plane separation
Playwright-based crawling engine with incremental portal discovery
MongoDB document storage with normalized procurement intelligence records

projects.name: Automated DevSecOps CI/CD and Deployment Pipeline System
projects.description: A controlled, state-driven execution pipeline that unifies the entire software delivery lifecycle into a multi-stage quality gate system. The pipeline enforces mandatory validation checkpoints from code commit through production release with automated security scanning and testing.
business_impact: Faster and more predictable deployments
Reduced production failures and rollback incidents
Early detection of security and dependency vulnerabilities
Standardized release process across teams and environments
Full traceability from code commit to production release
Reduced manual intervention in deployment workflows
engineering_highlights: State transition pipeline: Task Created → Code Commit → Build Stage → Security Validation → Testing Layer → Deployment Stage → Production Release
Five execution planes: Control Plane (Redmine, GitLab), Execution Plane (Jenkins), Validation Plane (SonarQube, Snyk, Gitleaks), Testing Plane (Cypress, Newman, JMeter), Deployment Plane (Docker)
Event-driven pipeline execution triggered by GitLab commits
Multi-stage quality gates with mandatory validation checkpoints
Parallel validation execution (API, UI, performance testing) to reduce feedback cycle time
Containerized deployment strategy for reproducible environments
Policy-based release control with enforced quality gates

projects.name: Event-Driven Processing Platform on AWS
projects.description: A production-pattern event processing system built on AWS managed infrastructure that decouples event ingestion from background processing using an asynchronous, queue-based architecture. The system handles burst traffic gracefully, isolates failures, and provides production-grade observability across independently scalable services.
business_impact: Handles burst traffic gracefully without degrading API latency
Isolates failures to prevent cascading system-wide issues
Provides real-time user feedback through Server-Sent Events
Production-grade observability across distributed services
Cost-effective through Fargate Spot with SQS durability
engineering_highlights: Asynchronous event pipeline: API publishes to SNS → SQS durable buffer → Worker processes → Redis pub/sub → SSE delivery
Backend Service (Express.js on ECS Fargate) for REST endpoints and SSE server
Worker Service (Node.js on ECS Fargate) with circuit breakers and bounded retry logic
SQS with Dead Letter Queue for poison message handling
Redis dual role: session cache and pub/sub event bus for real-time broadcasting
OpenTelemetry instrumentation with ADOT Collector sidecar exporting to AWS X-Ray
Prometheus metrics from both services for per-job-type SLA measurement
Dual logging strategy: CloudWatch Logs (compliance) and Loki/Promtail (developer experience)
Security: AWS Secrets Manager for credentials, least-privilege IAM roles, no hardcoded secrets
Horizontal scaling at each layer without coordinated deployments

projects.name: Handyman Marketplace System (SaaS Product Platform)
projects.description: A multi-actor state transition engine that models service fulfillment as a controlled workflow rather than a traditional listing platform. The system enforces trust, workflow control, lifecycle management, and provider quality constraints through state-driven design.
business_impact: Structured and predictable hiring workflow
Reduced friction in service discovery and negotiation
Improved trust through verification and reviews
Higher quality service matching through system-controlled rules
Reduced spam and low-quality provider interactions
Scalable marketplace behavior through state-driven design
engineering_highlights: Multi-actor architecture: Client (creates demand), Craftsman (supplies service offers), Admin (controls verification and trust), System (enforces state transitions)
Job state machine: Created → Open → Negotiation → Locked → Execution → Completed → Archived
Offer lifecycle: Created → Pending → Accepted/Rejected → Withdrawn
Craftsman lifecycle: Unverified → Verified → Blocked/Declined
Subscription lifecycle: Inactive → Pending → Active → Expired
Rule-based matching engine (location, service category, verification state, subscription status, trust score)
Communication system scoped to job/offer context with state-bound messaging
Subscription as visibility and prioritization control mechanism
Trust system as first-class system constraint embedded into matching logic
Real-time communication via Socket.io with event-driven updates

projects.name: Tax Rebate Processing & Refund Management Platform
projects.description: A workflow-driven state management engine for tax processing that manages the full lifecycle of tax rebate applications through a structured processing pipeline. The system replaces manual tax filing and spreadsheet tracking with state-controlled financial workflows.
business_impact: Significantly reduced manual processing effort
Faster tax rebate evaluation and approval cycles
Improved visibility into application status
Standardized refund workflows across cases
Reduced errors in tax calculations and documentation handling
Improved operational control for staff teams
Scalable handling of high-volume tax applications
engineering_highlights: Core system flow: Customer Registration → Identity Verification → Tax Information Collection → Document Upload → Document Processing → Tax Calculation → Staff Review → Refund Approval → Refund Processing → Completed Case
Workflow state model: Registration Started → Profile Completed → Documents Submitted → Tax Calculation Completed → Under Review → Refund Approved → Refund Processed
State-controlled financial workflow engine with enforced transitions
Customer onboarding system with identity verification
Tax information engine for structured financial data capture
Document processing system with AI-based extraction (Mindee AI)
Tax calculation engine with configurable tax rules
Refund management system for approval and processing tracking
Staff operations dashboard for monitoring and review
Role-based system design (customer vs staff vs system engine)

projects.name: News Chunk (Intelligent Content System)
projects.description: An AI-driven content consolidation pipeline that unifies access to multiple news sources through intelligent classification and structured extraction. The system replaces manual browsing across multiple platforms with a unified timeline interface.
business_impact: Faster access to relevant news across multiple sources
Reduced time spent switching between platforms
Improved content discoverability through AI classification
Unified view of global information streams
Personalized filtering and saved content workflow
Structured consumption of unstructured information
engineering_highlights: Layered processing model: Input Layer (multi-source web scraping) → Processing Layer (REST APIs) → Intelligence Layer (AI classification) → Output Layer (unified timeline interface)
Article lifecycle: Raw → Classified → Indexed → Displayed → Archived
Multi-source scraper that extracts structured data from heterogeneous sources
AI classification engine for standardized categorization
Unified timeline system combining all sources into single chronological feed
Saved articles system with persistent bookmarking
Decoupled scraping layer from API layer for maintainability
Normalized content schema to handle heterogeneous inputs

projects.name: Nutrifactor Mobile Commerce & Customer Experience Platform
projects.description: A headless commerce orchestration layer over Shopify that provides a complete shopping experience through a native mobile application. The platform manages real-time state synchronization across mobile client and Shopify backend through GraphQL commerce APIs.
business_impact: Complete mobile commerce experience for health and wellness products
Real-time state synchronization across mobile client and Shopify backend
Streamlined product discovery and purchasing workflows
Personalized recommendations based on customer behavior
Cross-session shopping state persistence
engineering_highlights: Stateful commerce proxy architecture mediating between mobile interactions and Shopify infrastructure
Four primary state domains: Commerce State, Shopping State, Engagement State, Customer State
State synchronization with optimistic updates, retry mechanisms, cache invalidation, session persistence
Product discovery: featured sections, category collections, hierarchical browsing, full-text search, session-aware recently viewed, behavior-driven recommendations
Cart & checkout management as state transition: local cart → Shopify sync → quantity validation → checkout orchestration → order submission
Customer engagement: wishlist management, recently viewed products, product recommendations, push notifications
Personalization without backend ML using heuristic adaptation from behavior signals
Mobile-optimized state architecture with minimal friction between discovery and purchase

projects.name: Virtual Office (React) — Real-Time Workspace Management System
projects.description: A real-time workspace management system that simulates an internal office environment where teams collaborate through structured presence, task execution, and communication flows. The system extends traditional task systems by introducing a live workplace layer.
business_impact: Real-time visibility of employee availability and focus state
Structured workplace presence signals for better coordination
Context-aware communication within room-based interactions
Efficient coordination between managers and employees
Live operational flow with embedded task management
engineering_highlights: Presence-driven workflow architecture where employee availability state governs all interaction rules
Employee presence states: At Desk, Focus Time, On Break, Offline
Room interaction states: Access Request → Check Presence State → Enter Room or Knock Event
Virtual Room System with enter/knock mechanics gated by presence state
Task execution system with Kanban workflow (Assigned → In Progress → Review → Completed)
Communication layer with real-time chat, video calling, and announcement broadcasting
Admin desk panel for room visibility, access controls, employee monitoring
Notification engine triggered by presence changes, task assignments, room access requests
Activity logger for system event tracking and operational visibility
Real-time layer chosen over polling for sub-second presence fidelity

projects.name: CopyFlow — School Submission & Print Workflow Management System
projects.description: A role-based school workflow system designed to manage academic submissions, reviews, and printing in a structured and controlled lifecycle. The system replaces manual submission handling with a state-driven document workflow engine.
business_impact: Standardized submission processing across all school roles
Replaces manual communication with controlled review and approval
Automates state transitions for printed documents
Improves coordination between teachers, secretaries, and principals
Full visibility into academic document lifecycle
Eliminates risk of misplaced or untracked printed documents
engineering_highlights: Role-based document lifecycle engine with four actor types: Admin, Teacher, Secretary, Principal
Submission state machine: Pending → Approved (print) or Censored (needs revision) → Printed → Archived
State transitions controlled by role permissions rather than open access
Teacher submission flow: Create Submission → Fill Form → Upload Documents → Generate Instructions → Submit
Secretary processing flow: Review → Censored (needs revision) or Approved for Print → Print → Auto Archive
Principal monitoring flow: Weekly Calendar View → Select Day → View Lessons → View Submission Details
Auto-generated print instruction documents from submission metadata
Print queue management with censored submissions awaiting execution
Weekly calendar view linking daily lesson scheduling to related submissions
Immutable archive preserving academic record integrity

formal_education.degree: Bachelor of Science in Electrical Engineering
formal_education.institution: National University of Computer and Emerging Sciences (NUCES), Lahore, Pakistan
